words = [
    0x07230203, 0x00010000, 0x00000000, 0x0000000F, 0x00000000,
    0x00020011, 0x00000001,
    0x0003000E, 0x00000000, 0x00000001,
    0x0006000F, 0x00000004, 0x0000000B, 0x6E69616D, 0x0000000A, 0x00000009,
    0x00030010, 0x0000000B, 0x00000000,
    0x00040047, 0x00000009, 0x00000000, 0x00000004,
    0x00040047, 0x0000000A, 0x00000000, 0x00000000,
    0x00020013, 0x00000001,
    0x00030014, 0x00000002, 0x00000020,
    0x00040017, 0x00000003, 0x00000002, 0x00000003,
    0x00040017, 0x00000004, 0x00000002, 0x00000004,
    0x00040020, 0x00000005, 0x00000001, 0x00000003,
    0x00040020, 0x00000006, 0x00000003, 0x00000004,
    0x00030021, 0x00000007, 0x00000001,
    0x0004002B, 0x00000002, 0x00000008, 0x3F800000,
    0x0004003B, 0x00000005, 0x00000009, 0x00000001,
    0x0004003B, 0x00000006, 0x0000000A, 0x00000003,
    0x00050036, 0x00000001, 0x0000000B, 0x00000000, 0x00000007,
    0x000200F8, 0x0000000C,
    0x0004003D, 0x00000003, 0x0000000D, 0x00000009,
    0x00050050, 0x00000004, 0x0000000E, 0x0000000D, 0x00000008,
    0x0003003E, 0x0000000A, 0x0000000E,
    0x000100FD,
    0x00010038
]

def to_little_endian(word):
    # Convert 32-bit word to 4 bytes and swap
    bytes_val = word.to_bytes(4, byteorder='little')
    return bytes_val.hex()

hex_string = "".join(to_little_endian(word) for word in words)
print(hex_string)
